content.text:
✓ Completed (9 turns, 0 tool calls)
[Execution: elapsed=44.3s, turns=9, status=ok]

[Findings: Found how pi discovers custom provider models (Nyro) — via ~/.pi/agent/models.json with hot-reload on /model open. No separate Nyro config exists. No sync command — manual edit or async extension factory needed.]
Files: /Users/shivam94/.pi/agent/models.json, /Users/shivam94/.pi/agent/settings.json, /Users/shivam94/.hermes/node/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/docs/models.md, /Users/shivam94/.hermes/node/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/docs/custom-provider.md
Next: Add new model entry to providers.nyro.models array in ~/.pi/agent/models.json. Models appear in /model after reopening. For auto-sync, build async extension using registerProvider() with remote fetch.

Now check Nyro & Pi config files for existing setup.Found the key files. Let me also check for any nyro-specific config or CLI that manages models.Let me check Nyro's package or docs location and look for any "nyro" CLI or config patterns.## Answer

Pi discovers custom providers exclusively via `~/.pi/agent/models.json`. No separate Nyro config file exists—Nyro config IS the `nyro` entry inside pi's `models.json`. The file hot-reloads when you open `/model`—no restart or sync command needed.

---

## Evidence

### 1. Where pi reads custom provider models

**File:** `~/.pi/agent/models.json`
**Documented in:** `/Users/shivam94/.hermes/node/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/docs/models.md`

Quote from models.md:

> "Add custom providers and models (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, proxies) via `~/.pi/agent/models.json`."
> "The file reloads each time you open `/model`. Edit during session; no restart needed."

No `pi models` sync/refresh command exists. Pi reads `models.json` on demand when opening the model selector (`/model` or Ctrl+L).

### 2. Existing Nyro provider config (your current setup)

**File:** `~/.pi/agent/models.json`

```json
{
  "providers": {
    "nyro": {
      "baseUrl": "http://localhost:19530/v1",
      "api": "openai-completions",
      "apiKey": "nyro",
      "compat": {
        "sendSessionAffinityHeaders": true,
        "supportsLongCacheRetention": true,
        "requiresReasoningContentOnAssistantMessages": true,
        "thinkingFormat": "deepseek",
        "supportsDeveloperRole": false
      },
      "models": [
        { "id": "mimo-v2.5-vision", "name": "Mimo V2.5 Vision", ... },
        { "id": "mimo-v2.5-pro", "name": "Mimo V2.5 Pro", ... },
        { "id": "deepseek-v4-flash-2", "name": "DeepSeek V4 Flash", ... },
        { "id": "deepseek-v4-pro-2", "name": "DeepSeek V4 Pro", ... },
        { "id": "kimi-k2.6-2", "name": "Kimi K2.6", ... },
        { "id": "glm-5.1-2", "name": "GLM 5.1", ... },
        { "id": "hermes-gateway", "name": "Hermes Gateway", ... },
        { "id": "ares-gateway", "name": "Ares Gateway", ... },
        { "id": "Opencode-free-2", "name": "Opencode Free 2", ... },
        { "id": "opencode-free-1", "name": "Opencode Free 1", ... },
        { "id": "Qwen-3.6-35B-A3B-mlx", "name": "Qwen 3.6 35B MLX", ... },
        { "id": "Qwen3.6-27B-UD-mlx", "name": "Qwen 3.6 27B MLX", ... }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### 3. Nyro is default provider in settings

**File:** `~/.pi/agent/settings.json`

```json
{
  "defaultProvider": "nyro",
  "defaultModel": "deepseek-v4-flash-2",
  "defaultThinkingLevel": "high",
  "enabledModels": [
    "nyro/deepseek-v4-pro-2",
    "nyro/opencode-free-1",
    "nyro/deepseek-v4-flash-2",
    "nyro/mimo-v2.5-pro",
    "nyro/mimo-v2.5-vision",
    "nyro/kimi-k2.6-2",
    "nyro/Opencode-free-2"
  ]
}
```

### 4. Alternative: Dynamic model discovery via extension

**Documented in:** `/Users/shivam94/.hermes/node/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/docs/custom-provider.md`

If you want Nyro to auto-sync models (instead of editing `models.json` by hand), create a pi extension that fetches models from Nyro's API at startup using `pi.registerProvider()`:

```typescript
export default async function (pi: ExtensionAPI) {
  const response = await fetch("http://localhost:19530/v1/models");
  const payload = await response.json();

  pi.registerProvider("nyro", {
    baseUrl: "http://localhost:19530/v1",
    apiKey: "nyro",
    api: "openai-completions",
    models: payload.data.map((model) => ({
      id: model.id,
      name: model.name ?? model.id,
      // ...map fields
    })),
  });
}
```

Quote from custom-provider.md:

> "If the model list comes from a remote endpoint, use an async extension factory..."
> "This registers the fetched models before startup finishes."
> "pi waits for the factory before startup continues, so the provider is available during interactive startup and to `pi --list-models`."

### 5. No separate Nyro config file exists

Checked and confirmed absent:
- `~/.config/nyro/config.json` — ENOENT
- `~/.nyro/config.json` — ENOENT
- `~/.nyro.json` — ENOENT
- `~/nyro.config.json` — ENOENT
- `~/.pi/agent/npm/node_modules/nyro` — ENOENT
- `~/node_modules/nyro` — ENOENT

Nyro configuration lives entirely inside pi's `models.json`.

---

## Caveats

- **No auto-sync currently:** Models won't auto-update if Nyro adds new models server-side. You must either (a) edit `models.json` manually and reopen `/model`, or (b) build a pi extension with async factory that fetches from Nyro's `/v1/models` endpoint at startup.
- **Nyro API endpoint unknown:** The extension approach assumes Nyro exposes an OpenAI-compatible `/v1/models` endpoint at `http://localhost:19530/v1/models`. If Nyro uses a different model listing endpoint, adjust the fetch URL.
- **Hot-reload scope:** `models.json` reloads on `/model` open only. It does not auto-detect filesystem changes.

---

## Findings
- summary: Found how pi discovers custom provider models (Nyro) — via ~/.pi/agent/models.json with hot-reload on /model open. No separate Nyro config exists. No sync command — manual edit or async extension factory needed.
- key_files: [/Users/shivam94/.pi/agent/models.json, /Users/shivam94/.pi/agent/settings.json, /Users/shivam94/.hermes/node/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/docs/models.md, /Users/shivam94/.hermes/node/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/docs/custom-provider.md]
- issues: none
- recommendation: Add new model entry to providers.nyro.models array in ~/.pi/agent/models.json. Models appear in /model after reopening. For auto-sync, build async extension using registerProvider() with remote fetch.

content.thinking: The researcher found the answer. Let me summarize it clearly for the user in caveman mode.

content.text:
**Two options.** No sync command exists.

### Option 1 — Manual (immediate)

Add model entry to `providers.nyro.models` array in `~/.pi/agent/models.json`.

Then reopen `/model` — hot-reloads on open. No restart needed.

```json
{
  "id": "your-new-model-id",
  "name": "Your New Model Name",
  "provider": "nyro",
  "thinking": false,
  "vision": false,
  "maxTokens": 8192,
  "tags": ["chat"]
}
```

### Option 2 — Auto-sync (extension)

Build pi extension that fetches models from Nyro's `/v1/models` endpoint at startup using `pi.registerProvider()`. Auto-discovers new models without manual edits.

Docs: `/Users/shivam94/.hermes/node/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/docs/custom-provider.md`

---

**What model did you add?** I can add it to `models.json` now if you give me the id/name/capabilities.
